landing_page.label: The Business Traveler
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests high-frequency direct-booking flow with time-sensitive friction tolerance
behavioral_markers: Arrives pre-qualified; booking in <3 min is the expectation
Hates corporate rate validation friction
Searches specifically: 'INNSIDE Madrid' not 'Madrid hotels'
Hyper-sensitive to hidden fees (resort, parking) that break corporate expense policy
Will abandon direct and book via Amex GBT / Concur if friction exceeds 90 seconds
Loyalty recognition at booking step is expected
failure_modes_revealed: Corporate rate not clearly visible
Mandatory 2-factor auth on every visit
Unclear 'breakfast included' rate plans
Resort fee disclosed only after payment
Slow search results or loading spinners >4s

landing_page.label: The Family Vacationer
landing_page.role_archetype: 2 adults + 1-3 kids booking summer/half-term holiday, leisure-focused
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests family-specific needs: connecting rooms, kids amenities, all-inclusive clarity
behavioral_markers: Spends 15-30 minutes researching across multiple properties
Reads photos of pools, kids clubs, family rooms obsessively
Compulsively checks for 'kids eat free', connecting rooms, cots
Needs to see real family photos (not stock) to trust the property
Abandons if 'all-inclusive' scope is unclear (drinks? snacks? activities?)
Reads 20+ reviews before booking; filters by 'traveled with family'
failure_modes_revealed: No filter for 'family-friendly' rooms
Kids club hours / ages not disclosed on booking page
All-inclusive inclusions buried in T&Cs
No connecting room option visible
Crib/cot availability unclear
No family pool / adults-only clarity

landing_page.label: The Luxury Seeker
landing_page.role_archetype: high-net-worth couple or solo, brand-conscious, expects premium service consistently
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests whether premium positioning holds across the funnel
behavioral_markers: Evaluates brand consistency: Gran Meliá vs Meliá vs Sol must feel distinct
Notices and is offended by stock photos, generic copy, crowded website
Expects concierge contact option / white-glove service signals
Sensitive to any cheap-looking element: dated buttons, promo badges
Compares to Four Seasons / Aman / Belmond reflexively
Will pay more for guaranteed bed type, high floor, view
failure_modes_revealed: Promotional banners cheapen premium brand feel
Rate comparisons with budget tier cannibalize luxury positioning
No concierge/VIP contact pre-booking option
Stock photos or inconsistent room photos
Generic descriptions indistinguishable from mid-tier brand

landing_page.label: The Honeymooner / Milestone Couple
landing_page.role_archetype: couple booking once-in-lifetime experience (honeymoon, anniversary, proposal)
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests emotional / romantic product positioning and upsell acceptance
behavioral_markers: Actively seeks 'romantic package', 'honeymoon offer', 'anniversary suite'
Reads reviews specifically from couples / honeymooners
Willing to upsell: suite upgrade, private dinner, spa couples' treatment
Emotional language signals matter: 'unforgettable', 'intimate', 'breathtaking'
Sensitive to family-focused imagery clashing with romance (kids in pool photos)
Wants adults-only floors/amenities, late dinner options
failure_modes_revealed: No distinct honeymoon/romance package visible
Property imagery mixes kids and couples (romance broken)
No adults-only options clearly marked
Upsell timing wrong (only at checkout, not pre-arrival)

landing_page.label: The Digital Nomad / Long-Stay
landing_page.role_archetype: remote worker staying 2-8 weeks, needs productivity and flexibility
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests long-stay product, workspace, weekly/monthly rates
behavioral_markers: Searches for 'desk in room', 'fast wifi', 'monthly rate', 'co-working'
Runs wifi speedtests on reviews; cross-references with Nomad List
Budget-aware; compares to Airbnb Plus and serviced apartments
Needs flexibility on check-in/out (12 or 14 night adjustments)
Reads recent reviews (last 60 days) to assess real conditions
Laptop-first: mobile-only flows that hide info enrage them
failure_modes_revealed: No monthly / weekly rate visible
Wifi speed not disclosed (or marketing speak instead of numbers)
No mention of workspace in room details
Cancellation flexibility unclear for long stays
No laundry amenity visible

landing_page.label: The Budget Optimizer
landing_page.role_archetype: price-sensitive leisure traveler, comparison shopper across OTAs and direct
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests price transparency, OTA rate parity, and value perception
behavioral_markers: Always opens at least 3 tabs: direct + Booking.com + Expedia
Calculates true total (resort fees, taxes, parking, breakfast) per night
Will abandon direct if €5/night more than OTA
Scrolls reviews looking for 'surprise fees' complaints
Sorts by price ascending then re-reads top 3
Will redeem loyalty points if it saves €20+
failure_modes_revealed: Direct rate higher than OTA (rate parity failure)
Resort/parking/breakfast fees not in nightly rate displayed
'Best price guarantee' claim not enforced or unclear
No loyalty benefit visible pre-booking
Tax/fee calculation hidden until checkout step

landing_page.label: The Loyalty Maximizer
landing_page.role_archetype: elite MeliáRewards / Bonvoy / Honors member, benefit-seeking, status-sensitive
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests whether elite recognition and benefits land at every touchpoint
behavioral_markers: Logs in before anything to see member rates and benefits
Scrutinizes 'Member-only' rates for real discount (not 1%)
Expects: bonus points display, free breakfast if applicable, upgrade eligibility
Will call customer service if recognition fails on booking page
Compares cost in points vs cash for high-value stays
Knows the program rules better than some call-center agents
failure_modes_revealed: Logged-in state not reflected in rates shown
Tier benefits not explicitly listed during booking
No clear 'points earned on this stay' estimate
Member rate discount trivial or deceptive
No points-cash toggle at booking

landing_page.label: The Event Attendee
landing_page.role_archetype: booking for conference / wedding / sports event; location and dates non-negotiable
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests group rates, location specificity, and constrained-date flexibility
behavioral_markers: Searches by exact dates with venue name
Sensitive to distance from venue (walking / taxi / shuttle)
Looks for 'group booking code' or wedding block field
Wants late checkout / early checkin on event day
Will pay premium for proximity; abandons if distance > 15 min
Reads check-in/check-out times carefully
failure_modes_revealed: No walking distance / transport info to major venues
No group code / block rate field
Unclear shuttle or transport options
Inflexible check-in/out times

onboarding.label: The First-Time Enroller
onboarding.role_archetype: just booked, prompted to join MeliáRewards
onboarding.coverage_purpose: Tests whether loyalty enrollment friction is below abandonment threshold
behavioral_markers: Will sign up if friction is <60 seconds
Wants immediate visible benefit (10% off next stay, bonus points)
Abandons if asked for phone / address / preferences before confirming signup
Doesn't read T&Cs; checks box if present
failure_modes_revealed: Too many required fields at signup
No immediate benefit shown
Terms & conditions blocking primary CTA

marketing_campaign.label: The In-Market Traveler
marketing_campaign.role_archetype: actively planning a trip, triggered by campaign
behavioral_markers: Reads headline first, dates second, price third
Ad-to-landing message match is critical (dest/dates/price must align)
Sensitive to bait-and-switch (ad says €80, landing shows €180)
failure_modes_revealed: Ad-landing price mismatch
No apparent continuity between ad creative and landing
Generic homepage instead of dedicated campaign LP

marketing_campaign.label: The Browsing Dreamer
marketing_campaign.role_archetype: not in-market but inspired by campaign
marketing_campaign.coverage_purpose: Tests brand attention and demand generation
behavioral_markers: Doesn't want to book now; saves for later or signs up for emails
Responds to inspirational / lifestyle imagery
Will share on social if compelling
failure_modes_revealed: No 'save for later' / email signup option
Only hard-sell CTAs; no soft conversion path
